description: Trigger and behavior cases for automation architecture: tool selection with rationale, structure, isolation/parallelization, reporting, CI placement, flake policy, migration steps.

cases.prompt: Design our test automation setup — we have a Vite/React app with a Postgres backend and basically no test infrastructure.

assertions: Selects tools per layer with rationale and a named rejected alternative, biased to incumbent tooling.
Designs directory structure, fixture/factory placement, import boundaries, and an auth-state strategy.
Defines parallel-safe isolation (per-worker data namespacing, DB strategy) and CI tiers with runtime budgets and a bounded, logged retry policy.
Delivers an ordered migration plan of independently shippable steps and hands test implementation to the engineer skills.

cases.prompt: Our test suite takes 45 minutes, can't run in parallel because tests share seed users, and CI retries everything three times. Fix the architecture.

assertions: Diagnoses shared mutable fixtures as the parallelization blocker and designs per-worker namespacing (composing test-data-architect for data design).
Replaces unbounded retries with a bounded, logged policy routed to flaky-test-detective.
Proposes sharding/tiering against runtime budgets rather than a big-bang rewrite.

cases.prompt: What should we test in the new subscription flow before launch?

assertions: Deciding WHAT to test for a change is test-plan-designer (with qa-strategy-architect above it); this skill designs the automation machinery.

cases.prompt: Set up the automation architecture and then write the full Playwright suite for all our flows.

assertions: Delivers the blueprint, then hands Playwright implementation to playwright-e2e-engineer (manual-only) instead of writing specs in the same pass.
